description: Construct a triangle from two angles and a non-included side (Angle-Angle-Side). The third angle is computed as 180 - angleA - angleB. Remaining sides are derived via the law of sines. Vertex A is at the base point (or origin if omitted).
utility: Create triangle from two angles and a non-included side opposite the first angle

arguments.name: angleA
arguments.description: Angle at vertex A in degrees. Must be positive.

arguments.name: angleB
arguments.description: Angle at vertex B in degrees. angleA + angleB must be less than 180.

arguments.name: a
arguments.description: Length of side opposite to angle A (from B to C). Must be positive.

arguments.name: basePoint
arguments.description: Position for vertex A (defaults to origin). Must be a point variable or point(G, x, y).

arguments.name: angle
arguments.description: Rotation angle in degrees for the entire triangle around vertex A

alternates.description: Triangle at origin with two angles and opposite side
Special right triangle with known angles

examples.scenario: AAS triangle with two given angles and opposite side
Extract and measure the given side a
examples.expression: line_a = line(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(edge), 2), type(segment), c(blue))
